items.title: Six arrested during England’s World Cup clash with Croatia as cops reveal two fights broke out in stands
items.summary: TEXAS COPS confirmed six people were arrested during England's 4-2 World Cup win over Croatia - including one for criminal trespass. items.title: Tuchel: Saka unlikely to start against Ghana
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel says Bukayo Saka will be nursed through the next week of training but is unlikely to start for England until the final World Cup group game against Panama.

items.title: Neville: France still favourites despite England display
items.summary: Gary Neville believes England produced the best football of the World Cup so far in their second-half display against Croatia, but remains unconvinced that Thomas Tuchel's side should be considered favourites for the tournament.

items.title: Advantage NZ as England wickets tumble after maiden Phillips ton
items.summary: New Zealand claimed the key wickets of Joe Root and Harry Brook to seize control of the second Test against England after Glenn Phillips' maiden Test century helped the tourists build a commanding first-innings lead at The Oval.

items.title: Brazil's Neymar (calf injury) also out vs. Haiti
items.summary: Neymar will not play or travel with the Brazil team to Philadelphia for their FIFA World Cup Group C match against Haiti, the Brazil federation confirmed Thursday.

items.title: Sources: Real Madrid admire Chelsea's Fernández
